variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pop/pop.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is a non-negative integer (may be up to 64-bit unsigned, i.e. up to 2^63-1). For each, output the number of set bits on its own line. Implement popcount yourself — no language-builtin popcount intrinsic. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pop; ! grep -qE "\\.bit_count\\(|bin\\(.*\\)\\.count\\(" pop.py || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; python3 pop.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pop/pop.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is a non-negative integer (may be up to 64-bit unsigned, i.e. up to 2^63-1). For each, output the number of set bits on its own line. Implement popcount yourself — no language-builtin popcount intrinsic. Compile with `go build -o pop pop.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pop; ! grep -qE "bits\\.OnesCount" pop.go || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; go build -o pop pop.go && ./pop < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pop/pop.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each next line is a non-negative integer (may be up to 64-bit unsigned, i.e. up to 2^63-1). For each, output the number of set bits on its own line. Implement popcount yourself — no language-builtin popcount intrinsic. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast pop.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pop; ! grep -qE "@popCount" pop.zig || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast pop.zig && ./pop < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
